title: Azgoghk

The '''''Azgoghk''''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Prison ship/Legends|dungeon ship]] created for the extermination of [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Species/Legends|species]] in the farthest reaches of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], in accordance with [[High Human Culture/Legends|Human High Culture]]. Part of a trio of [[Imperial extermination ship|extermination ships]] commissioned especially to serve rabidly [[Humanocentrism|Humanocentric]] Imperial [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Danetta Pitta]], it was [[Commander/Legends|commanded]] by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Mir Tork]], with chief [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Leonis Murthé]] serving as second-in-command. Along with the ''[[Angrix]]'' and ''[[Apocahk]]'', the ''Azgoghk'' and its considerable escort forces were deployed to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], officially on an Imperial exploration mission, in truth to locate, [[Slavery/Legends|enslave]] and eradicate non-[[Human/Legends|Human]] civilizations.

For several years the ''Azgoghk'' roamed the Rim, harvesting [[Planet/Legends|planets]] of their resources and populace; however, the dungeon ship's escort force was called to [[Battle of Endor/Legends|fight]] at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], and the vulnerable ''Azgoghk'' was disabled by [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]] forces. Murthé and Tork escaped, and later returned to the site of the intact ''Azgoghk'' and resumed their mandate, even though Pitta was [[Death/Legends|dead]] and the Empire was in disarray.

One of the last species targeted by the ship, the [[Gulmarid]]s, hired [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] to execute Murthé and Tork; Fett engaged with the ''Azgoghk'' on [[Malicar 3]], disabling it and killing its two insane overseers. The ship remained dormant on Malicar 3, its final batch of prisoners freed by Fett; the bounty hunter returned the heads of Murthé and Tork to Gulmarid survivor [[Slique Brighteyes]].

==Description==
[[File:Azgoghk.jpg|thumb|left|150px|The [[Gulmarid]]s being led to their deaths.]]
The death ship was commanded by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Mir Tork]] and his sadistic science officer [[Leonis Murthé]], and was one of three such ships, along with the ''[[Apocahk]]'' and ''[[Angrix]]'', under the banner of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Danetta Pitta]].

==History==
==="Exploration"===
The ''Azgoghk'', alongside its sister ships ''Angrix'' and ''Apocahk'', came to prominence during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], at some point after [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]]. [[Danetta Pitta]] was appointed an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] in that year; a rabid, [[Speciesism|anti-alien]] bigot—who, ironically, was himself of [[near-Human]] descent—he was a staunch believer in [[High Human Culture/Legends|Human High Culture]]. Pitta advocated the extermination of non-Human species, and to this end he was given control of the three dungeon ships. Officially they were used for exploration and expanding the Empire's borders in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], but Pitta used them solely for genocide.<ref name="who's who">{{InsiderCite|66|Who's Who: Imperial Grand Admirals}}</ref> Each ship worked separately, with a large escort force consisting of several [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyers]] and other, smaller support craft. Once a planet's species was wiped out and its resources stripped bare, each ship would move on to another world ripe for exploitation.<ref name="aod" />

The ''Azgoghk'' was commanded by [[Mir Tork]], a ruthlessly efficient, fanatically [[Humanocentrism|Humanocentric]] Imperial [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]]. Second-in-command was chief [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Leonis Murthé]], another whose hatred for aliens was matched only by Tork and Pitta. The ship wiped out hundreds of species on its travels, while Murthé performed experiments on choice captives, prolonging their misery before eventually killing them.<ref name="aod" />

===Hiatus===
Several months prior to the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], the Empire took the remote, primitive world of [[Gulma]], and the ''Azgoghk'' was sent to the surface to vacuum up its inhabitants, the peaceful [[Gulmarid]]. A force of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] rounded up the population, informing them that they were to be taken offworld for their own safety. Inquisitive Gulmarid were brutally punished, and the naïve species acquiesced to the Imperial demands and boarded the ship. The ship was later abandoned by the imperials and was liberated by rebel fighters. Mir Tork and Leonis Murthe later returned to the ship with a force of slavers.<ref name="aod" />

===Post-Endor===
One survivor of the ''Azgoghk's'' cleansing was a Gulmarid named [[Slique Brighteyes]], who hired [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] in [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]] to kill Tork and Murthé in exchange for the Gulmarids' combined wealth: 100 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. Ostensibly to improve his damaged reputation (following his defeat at the [[Great Pit of Carkoon/Legends|Great Pit of Carkoon]]), Fett accepted the job. On [[Malicar 3]], Fett single-handedly brought down the ship with an [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]]. Fett then infiltrated the ship and killed Tork and Murthé. He also freed dozens of alien prisoners, who stripped weapons from Fett's trail of corpses and revolted against the remaining slavers.

Fett returned to Brighteyes on [[Basteel/Legends|Basteel]] with the severed heads of Tork and Murthé, having completed his assignment. The ''Azgoghk's'' reign of terror had finally ended.

===Fett===
[[File:Malicar_3.jpg|thumb|right|220px|[[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] engages the ''Azgoghk''.]]

The Gulmarids, one the last species captured by the ''Azgoghk'' pre-Endor, were slowly dying out; those who had escaped the ''Azgoghk'' were in terrible physical condition and many simply lost the will to live. The survivors, represented by [[Slique Brighteyes]], were determined that Tork and Murthé would die before the last of the Gulmarids did. Pooling together their resources, which totaled a miserly one hundred [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]], they decided to put out a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on Tork and Murthé. Prices had previously been put on the pair's heads,<ref name="aod" /> but [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]] had either died trying<ref name="ff" /> or refused to touch the contract, believing that the ''Azgoghk'' was impenetrable.<ref name="aod" /> Brighteyes, however, sought to hire [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]]; once the most notorious bounty hunter in the galaxy, Fett's career was stuttering after a number of high profile failures, and Brighteyes hoped he would accept the mission to prove his abilities.<ref name="ff" />

Brighteyes outlined his proposition to Fett, but when the Gulmarid informed Fett of the reward, the bounty hunter almost killed him on the spot for wasting his time. However, after further consideration Fett accepted the offer,<ref name="aod" /> in a last ditch attempt to reignite his failing career. The ''Azgoghk'' mission was said to be one of the most bizarre bounties ever accepted by Fett, and in a galaxy rife with Fett impostors, some questioned whether it was even Fett who undertook it.<ref name="ff" />
